Home_SafetySub: Before SIPs and loans, know these Government of India covers — very low yearly cost, useful for almost every working adult with a bank account.
Home_SafetyHealthFoot: ₹2L is a floor — estimate what your family may need
Home_SafetyCta: Read full guide: eligibility, deduction & nominee claims
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_Subtitle: Low-cost government accident and life cover
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_Description: Plain-language guide to PMSBY and PMJJBY — eligibility, auto-debit, multiple banks, missing a year, and how nominees receive the claim.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_EduNote: Educational summary based on Government of India scheme rules. Premiums and terms can change — confirm with your bank or the official Jan Suraksha portal before enrolling.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_PmsbyAbout: A one-year personal accident insurance scheme, renewable every year. It pays for accidental death or disability — not for illness or natural death.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_PmsbyPremium: Annual premium: about {0} (auto-debit from your bank / Post Office account).
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_PmsbyCover: Cover: {0} for accidental death or permanent total disability; ₹1 lakh for permanent partial disability.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_PmsbyAge: Age to join: 18 to 70 years (with an eligible savings account).
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_PmsbyPeriod: Cover year: usually 1 June to 31 May.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_PmjjbyName: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_PmjjbyAbout: A one-year pure term life cover, renewable every year. It pays on death due to any cause (subject to scheme rules and the initial lien period). There is no maturity or investment return.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_PmjjbyPremium: Annual premium: about {0} (auto-debit in one instalment).
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_PmjjbyCover: Cover: {0} paid to the nominee on the insured person’s death.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_PmjjbyAge: Age to join: 18 to 50 years; cover can usually continue with renewals up to 55.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_PmjjbyPeriod: Cover year: usually 1 June to 31 May.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_TogetherBody: Together they cost about {0} a year and can provide up to {1} accident cover (PMSBY) plus {2} life cover (PMJJBY). They are a low-cost floor — not a full health policy or a full term plan for higher income needs.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_Q01: What exactly do these policies cover?
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_A01: PMSBY covers accidental death and disability only. PMJJBY covers death due to any cause (with a short waiting/lien period for non-accidental death on fresh enrolment or re-joining). Neither pays hospital bills for illness — that needs separate health insurance (or schemes like Ayushman Bharat where eligible).
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_A02: You need an individual savings account (or eligible joint account) with a participating bank or Post Office in India, and you must give consent for auto-debit. PMSBY: age 18–70. PMJJBY: age 18–50 to join. Aadhaar is typically used as primary KYC for the account. NRIs with an eligible India-branch account may join, but claim payout is in Indian rupees.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_A03: At enrolment you sign a consent / auto-debit form. The bank or Post Office deducts the yearly premium in one instalment from the linked account (often around May–June for the next cover year). Keep enough balance so the debit succeeds — failed debit usually means cover does not start or renew for that year.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_Q04: If multiple banks deduct the premium, do I get ₹2 lakh from each bank?
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_A04: No. Official rules allow enrolment through only one bank or Post Office account per person for each scheme. Even if premium is wrongly deducted from more than one account, you (or your nominee) are eligible for only one claim. Extra premiums are generally not treated as extra cover — contact the banks to stop duplicate enrolments.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_Q05: If I miss taking it this year, can I take it next year?
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_A05: Yes. These are yearly renewable schemes. If you skip a year or your cover lapses, you can usually enrol again later while you still meet the age and account rules. For PMJJBY, fresh enrolment or re-joining after a break typically restarts a short lien period for non-accidental death (commonly 30 days from enrolment under revised rules). Accidental death under PMJJBY is generally covered from day one of cover.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_A06: Yes, delayed enrolment is usually allowed. Cover then typically starts from the date of successful auto-debit and runs until 31 May. PMJJBY may charge a pro-rata premium depending on the quarter you join; renewals for a full year usually need the full annual premium.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_Q07: How does the nominee get the money?
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_A07: Name a nominee when you enrol (and keep that nomination updated). On a claim, the nominee approaches the same bank / Post Office branch where the scheme was linked, submits the claim form with required documents, and the insurer settles the approved amount into the nominee’s bank account (or legal heirs’ account if there is no valid nomination).
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_Q08: What documents are usually needed for a claim?
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_A08: Typically: filled claim form from the bank/insurer, death certificate (for death claims), identity and relationship proof of the nominee, and nominee bank account details. For PMSBY disability claims, medical/disability certificates as required by the insurer. The bank verifies enrolment and forwards papers to the insurance company.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_Q09: In a joint account, can every holder take the cover?
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_A09: Yes, if each person meets eligibility and pays their own premium via auto-debit. Each life is enrolled separately — one person’s cover does not automatically protect the other joint holders.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_Q10: Is ₹2 lakh enough for my family?
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_A10: Treat it as a basic safety net, not complete protection. Hospital costs and income replacement for many families need higher health and term cover. Use FinYatra’s Health Insurance and Term Insurance calculators to estimate a fuller need after you understand these schemes.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_Q11: Do I get money back if I don’t claim?
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_A11: No. Both are pure risk covers for that year. There is no maturity value, no savings component, and no refund of premium if you stay healthy and do not claim.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_Q12: Are these mandatory for every job?
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_A12: No. They are voluntary government-backed schemes. FinYatra highlights them because the cost is very low and awareness helps — not because any employer or law forces every worker to buy them. Some workplaces may facilitate enrolment; you still choose and consent.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_Q13: How is this different from e-Shram or Ayushman Bharat?
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_A13: e-Shram is a worker registration portal that can help unorganised workers discover linked benefits; PMSBY/PMJJBY themselves are bank/Post Office insurance products. Ayushman Bharat (PM-JAY) is health cover for eligible families — different from PMSBY accident cover and PMJJBY life cover.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_Q14: Where should I enrol and verify the latest premium?
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_A14: Enrol through your bank branch, net banking / mobile banking (if offered), or Post Office where the scheme is available. Premiums and fine print can be revised by the government — always confirm the current amount and rules with your bank or on the official Jan Suraksha website before relying on any number.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_HowTo1: Choose one savings account at a participating bank or Post Office (avoid duplicate enrolments).
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_HowTo2: Fill the PMSBY and/or PMJJBY enrolment cum auto-debit consent form and name your nominee.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_HowTo3: Keep enough balance for the yearly debit; note the cover year (typically 1 June–31 May).
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_HowTo4: Save the acknowledgement and tell your nominee which bank holds the cover.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_NextTitle: After this floor — plan fuller cover
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_NextBody: Use these schemes as awareness and a low-cost base. Then estimate health and term needs with FinYatra tools, or explore e-Shram-linked benefit notes if you are an unorganised worker.
Page_PmsbyPmjjby_DisclaimerBody: FinYatra is educational only — not a bank, insurer, or government portal. We do not enrol you, collect premium, or settle claims. Scheme rules, premiums, lien periods, and claim processes are set by the Government of India and participating insurers/banks and may change. Always verify with your bank, Post Office, or official sources before deciding.
